title: Test Pipeline on Core Herbs Batch
content:
## Context
The improved 4-stage content pipeline successfully removed blog voice and preserved citations on tickly-cough protocol. Ready to scale.

## Prompts Ready
- /home/patrick/projects/folkprotocol/prompts/stage1-bibliography-prompt.md
- /home/patrick/projects/folkprotocol/prompts/stage2-research-summary-prompt.md  
- /home/patrick/projects/folkprotocol/prompts/stage3-synthesis-improved.md
- /home/patrick/projects/folkprotocol/prompts/stage4-qc-edit-prompt.md

## Test Batch
Run these 5 herbs through the pipeline:
1. Rhodiola (adaptogen, well-studied)
2. Elderberry (immune, strong evidence)
3. Passionflower (anxiety/sleep, moderate evidence)
4. Ginger (nausea/digestive, extensive use)
5. Echinacea (immune, controversial evidence)

## Launch Pattern
```python
herbs = ["rhodiola", "elderberry", "passionflower", "ginger", "echinacea"]
for herb in herbs:
    # Stage 1: Bibliography
    bib_id = spin(f"Create {herb} bibliography", prompt_file="stage1", permission="shard", model="sonnet")
    # Wait then continue stages...
```

## Success Metrics
- Citation density >80%
- Zero blog phrases
- All numbers preserved from research
- Traditional claims have sources
- Cross-references working
